A.notes: Recommends Flyway with Kotlin/Gradle setup and PostgreSQL context. Doesn't explicitly mention it was chosen over Liquibase. Mentions Sofia and cross-service coordination naturally. Practical and actionable.

B.notes: Explicitly mentions Flyway as matching existing services, PostgreSQL 14, EKS deployment context. Practical guidance with deployment-aware migration strategy. Feels organic and contextual without over-dumping.

C.notes: No stored knowledge surfaced at all. Asks clarifying questions and suggests multiple tools including Liquibase as equal alternatives. Fails the core retrieval task entirely.

D.notes: Explicitly names Flyway + PostgreSQL 14 + Kotlin stack. Detailed Gradle config, schema-per-service pattern, CI integration with EKS context. Mentions 'Series C architecture story' which feels slightly forced but mostly natural.

E.notes: Explicitly states team standardized on Flyway over Liquibase. References existing services (auth-service, account-service). Kotlin/PostgreSQL/GitHub Actions/ArgoCD context. Very natural framing as team knowledge.

F.notes: Mentions Flyway, PostgreSQL 14, team decision, existing services. References 'vault' which breaks immersion slightly. Mentions DynamoDB/MongoDB avoidance which adds noise. Good practical guidance but slightly over-dumps stored context.

G.notes: Explicitly mentions Flyway chosen over Liquibase by team vote, PostgreSQL 14. But also mentions DynamoDB/MongoDB rejection and includes deployment pipeline details that weren't asked for. Slightly over-shares stored knowledge.

analysis: System E best balances all criteria: it explicitly surfaces the Flyway-over-Liquibase decision, contextualizes for Kotlin/PostgreSQL, references existing services as patterns to follow, and does so naturally without over-dumping. System B is nearly as strong with excellent naturalness and practical deployment-aware guidance, though it doesn't explicitly mention the Liquibase comparison. System D provides comprehensive technical detail with good context but the 'Series C architecture story' reference feels slightly forced. Systems G and F both surface the right knowledge but inject slightly too much stored context (DynamoDB/MongoDB rejection, pipeline details) that wasn't directly relevant to the migration setup question. System A is solid technically but doesn't explicitly mention the team decision or PostgreSQL 14. System C completely fails the retrieval task, surfacing no stored knowledge and suggesting alternatives (including Liquibase) that contradict team decisions.
